text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared main repository. After every merge, an automated CI pipeline starts. This pipeline first runs automated builds to compile the new code and check for errors. Then, it automatically runs a suite of tests, including unit and integration tests, to verify that the new code works correctly with the existing codebase. A key advantage of CI is catching issues early, right after each change, before they become bigger, harder problems. This approach helps teams maintain code quality and fix bugs quickly, making the entire development process more efficient and reliable.
